api_contract.backward_compatibility: Introduce as new node type `gmailTrigger` without changing existing trigger contracts. Payload adds Gmail-specific object under stable top-level event envelope; unknown fields are additive only. Existing workflow engine semantics (at-least-once delivery, retry model) are preserved.
error_contract: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR: invalid filter/query/limits with field-level details
403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE: required Gmail scope not granted
409 CONFLICT: trigger already exists for same node+credential when uniqueness enforced
429 RATE_LIMITED: quota exceeded, includes retryAfter hint
503 RETRY_SCHEDULED: temporary activation/dependency failure; activation pending

architecture_overview: Add a Gmail Trigger subsystem with two ingestion paths (Gmail Watch via Pub/Sub push and polling fallback) behind one trigger runtime interface. A Gmail connector service handles OAuth2 token refresh, Gmail API calls (watch/history/messages/attachments), and normalization. Trigger state service persists checkpoint (historyId/cursor), dedup keys, health, and renewal metadata. A scheduler manages polling cadence and watch renewal. Event emission is idempotent and at-least-once, with dedup on messageId+historyId and replay-safe processing. Secrets remain in encrypted credential storage; observability exposes status, lag, renewal, retries, and quota errors.
assumptions: Workflow platform already supports generic trigger lifecycle (create/activate/pause/resume/delete) and job queue execution.
Encrypted credential store exists and can reference OAuth secrets by credentialId.
A persistent relational datastore with transactional guarantees is available.
System can expose/receive authenticated HTTP endpoints for Pub/Sub push callbacks.
v1 parity target is behavioral similarity to n8n, not byte-for-byte payload identity.
Default activation behavior is no historical backfill unless user opts in.
constraints: Must not log tokens, message bodies, or attachment bytes unless explicitly permitted by secure debug mode.
Use least-privilege Gmail scopes (prefer gmail.readonly) and avoid broader scopes in v1.
Event delivery target is at-least-once; dedup must provide effectively-once behavior for downstream workflows.
Watch mode requires external Pub/Sub + public push endpoint; polling must remain fully functional as fallback.
Attachment downloads must enforce max size and streaming limits to avoid memory spikes.
Checkpoint updates and emissions must be transactionally ordered to prevent missed/duplicate events on crash.
db_changes: Create `gmail_trigger_configs` (trigger_id FK, mode, filters JSONB, attachment_policy, poll_interval_sec, backfill_policy, include_spam_trash, mailbox_scope, enabled flags).
Create `gmail_trigger_state` (trigger_id PK, checkpoint_history_id, cursor_ts, last_processed_internal_date, watch_expiration_ts, renewal_attempts, last_success_ts, last_error_code, paused_reason).
Create `gmail_dedup` (trigger_id, dedup_key, first_seen_ts, ttl_ts) with unique index on (trigger_id, dedup_key).
Create `gmail_dead_letters` (id, trigger_id, source_message_id, error_type, error_payload, attempts, created_ts, last_attempt_ts, resolved_ts).
Add metrics rollup table or stream labels for trigger_id: processed_count, duplicate_count, retry_count, auth_fail_count, quota_fail_count, lag_ms.
implementation_steps: Define node schema/UI/DSL for Gmail Trigger config with validation defaults and field-level errors.
Integrate Google OAuth2 credential flow for offline access and encrypted refresh token storage; implement refresh-token rotation handling.
Build Gmail API adapter methods: watch.start/stop, history.list pagination, messages.get (format full/metadata), attachments.get streaming.
Implement normalization mapper to stable event payload (ids, headers, snippet, body parts, labels, internalDate, attachments metadata/content by policy).
Implement state/checkpoint manager with atomic update and dedup insert semantics to ensure idempotent emission.
Implement watch ingestion endpoint and verification, queue handoff, duplicate/out-of-order notification handling.
Implement polling worker with query construction, windowing, incremental cursoring, and overlap-safe dedup.
Add retry/backoff policy and terminal error classification (auth revoked, invalid historyId, bad config).
Implement invalid historyId recovery path (controlled resync from bounded window or configured backfill policy).
Add scheduler jobs for polling cadence and watch renewal; support auto-fallback mode logic.
Instrument logs/metrics/status endpoints and expose trigger health/checkpoint/watch renewal state in UI/API.
Write docs and run integration tests covering OAuth lifecycle, filtering, duplicates, restart recovery, MIME/attachment edge cases, and fault injection.
request_flow: User creates Gmail Trigger node config (mode, filters, attachment policy) and links OAuth2 credential.
Backend validates config, stores node config, stores encrypted refresh token reference, initializes checkpoint state.
If mode=watch: backend calls users.watch, stores watch expiration/historyId, and subscribes Pub/Sub push endpoint mapping to trigger instance.
Pub/Sub notification arrives with emailAddress/historyId; receiver authenticates source, enqueues trigger job.
Worker loads checkpoint, calls Gmail history.list from last historyId, fetches candidate message IDs, dedups, hydrates messages, applies filters/policies, emits workflow events, updates checkpoint.
If mode=poll: scheduler enqueues periodic job; worker queries messages.list using configured query/labels and time cursor, then same dedup/hydrate/emit/update path.
On retriable failures (429/5xx/network), worker retries with exponential backoff and jitter; on terminal auth/config errors, trigger is paused/error-state with actionable reason.
Watch renewal job runs before expiration; if renewal fails repeatedly, auto-fallback to polling (if enabled) or pause with status.
